title: Say The Line, Sam: Name The Movie Behind Each Iconic Jackson Quote
dek: Eight lines, zero movie titles — just Samuel L. Jackson's voice ringing in your head and your job to place it before we spill the answer.

items.quote: "The path of the righteous man is beset on all sides by the inequities of the selfish and the tyranny of evil men." — Jules Winnfield
items.reveal_text: That whole speech sounds biblical, but it's basically Tarantino fan-fiction — critics have pointed out it's <cite index="62-3">a fabricated biblical passage delivered with unwavering conviction</cite>, and it's what turned Jackson into a legend overnight.

items.quote: "Hold on to your butts." — Ray Arnold
items.reveal_text: This four-word classic wasn't even in the script. Screenwriter David Koepp has said <cite index="56-3">as the lights were going down, Bob Zemeckis said, 'Hold onto your butts.' I happened to be working on the script at that time, and I was like, 'Oh, I love that.' I went back and I typed it into the script immediately, and then Sam Jackson said it</cite>.

items.quote: "I have had it with these motherf***ing snakes on this motherf***ing plane!" — Neville Flynn
items.reveal_text: This line exists because the internet basically demanded it. Fans wanted the movie bumped to R so Jackson could really let loose, and <cite index="76-1,76-2">reshoots were ordered, and Snakes on a Plane was transformed from the PG-13 movie it was planned to be, into the R-rated movie we have today</cite>.

items.quote: "There was an idea, Stark knows this, called the Avengers Initiative." — Nick Fury
items.reveal_text: The full speech goes: <cite index="20-1,20-2,20-3,20-4">There was an idea, Stark knows this, called the Avengers Initiative. The idea was to bring together a group of remarkable people, to see if they could become something more. To see if they could work together when we needed them to. To fight the battles that we never could. Phil Coulson died still believing in that idea</cite>. It's the payoff to a tease Fury first dropped back in Iron Man's 2008 post-credits scene.

items.quote: "Not even God knows what you're doing, McClane!" — Zeus Carver
items.reveal_text: Believe it or not, this was actually a first: despite both appearing in Pulp Fiction the year before, <cite index="3-28,3-29">Jackson and Willis appeared in Loaded Weapon 1 (the latter had a cameo) and Pulp Fiction. However, this was the first time they had actually been on screen together before</cite>.

items.quote: "Yes, they deserved to die and I hope they burn in hell!" — Carl Lee Hailey
items.reveal_text: This courtroom outburst is widely considered one of Jackson's best dramatic moments, and it earned him real hardware: he took home the <cite index="53-31">NAACP Image Awards: Outstanding Actor</cite> for the role.

items.quote: "Just who the hell you callin' 'Snowball,' hoss boy?" — Stephen
items.reveal_text: Stephen is one of the most unsettling characters Jackson's ever played — reviewers have described him as <cite index="32-6">a figure of chilling loyalty and a kind of sinister intelligence</cite>, which makes every scene he's in land like a gut punch.

items.quote: "I came to coach basketball players, and you became students. I came to teach boys, and you became men." — Coach Ken Carter
items.reveal_text: The film is based on a real Richmond High coach who actually locked his undefeated team out of the gym over bad grades — a decision the movie leans into hard, with <cite index="8-12,8-13">Carter locks the gym and offers to open only when the team players deliver on their contract's terms. Carter's decision proves very unpopular</cite> with the whole town.
